resources.string: No recovery key was saved for this vault. The encrypted files can\'t be opened on this device anymore. Reset to start fresh.
Enter your recovery key to restore access to %1$d folder(s). You\'ll confirm with your device once per folder.
Save a recovery key now, or accept that losing this device — or re-enrolling your fingerprint — permanently destroys the vault.
Skip — I accept lost device means lost vault
Write this down and store it off this device. It is the only way to recover the vault after a fingerprint re-enrollment. We cannot recover it for you.
I saved this key somewhere safe
To keep an off-device copy, use the recovery key above, or export a file and store the plain copy where you choose.
Add this file to the vault?

%1$s
%2$s · %3$s

Adding into folder: %4$s. The file is encrypted into the vault under this folder's master key.
Preview not available for this type — export to view externally.
This build won\'t run on this device.
Required to release this folder\'s master key.
Required to seed this folder\'s master key.

Vault-folder generates its own 256-bit master key, self-encrypts it under a hardware-backed Keystore key, and self-binds it to this device\'s screen lock. Files you add are individually AES-256-GCM encrypted under that master.
Per-file size cap: 20 MiB. Vault-folder is for documents, keys, recovery codes, photos — not video archives.
Authenticate with your device to bind the vault master key.
Device screen lock required.\n\nVault-folder binds the master key to your device\'s PIN / pattern / biometric. Set up a screen lock in system Settings, then come back.
BiometricPrompt unavailable (status %1$d). Configure a strong biometric or device credential in system Settings.
Authenticate with your device biometric or PIN.
Nothing to export — file no longer available
No files yet. Tap Add file to import one.
Deleting permanently removes the encrypted blob from this vault. There is no recycle bin and no recovery path. Export first if you might want it back.
Pick a file via the system file picker. It\'s encrypted in-place under this vault\'s master key. Per-file cap: 20 MiB.
On — you\'ll confirm before each shred. Only works on URIs with WRITE permission (system file picker, not bare Open-with deposits).
Off — the source file stays where it is.
Added %1$s · shred failed: %2$s (your encrypted copy is safe; delete the source manually)

After encrypting this file into the vault, attempt to permanently delete the source file from where you picked it. This cannot be undone if it succeeds. If the source URI doesn\'t grant write access, the delete will fail and the source will remain — your encrypted copy is safe either way.
No recovery key was enrolled for this vault, so there is no recovery-encrypted backup to make. Use the per-file Export action in the list to save an individual file instead.
Enter your recovery key. The backup is encrypted so only this key can open it.
Each folder has its own master key, biometric-released. Switching folders prompts for biometric again. The Default folder is the one created at first setup.
Deletes the folder \"%1$s\" and ALL files in it. No recycle bin, no recovery.
Pick a name. Each folder is independently encrypted; you\'ll authenticate again after picking a name to seed the new folder\'s master key.
Authenticate to seed the new folder\'s master key.
